executive_summary: The system will allow users to create an account using an email address and a password. Email verification is required before the first login. User account data will be stored in a database. Password must be 8-12 characters, including at least one number, one special character, and one alphabet character. Email must be unique and cannot exceed 255 characters. Meaningful inline error messages will be displayed for validation failures.
functional_summary: The system supports user account creation with email and password. Email verification is mandatory before the first login. User account data is persisted in a database. Validation includes email uniqueness, email length (max 255), password length (8-12), and password complexity (at least one number, one special character, one alphabet). Inline error messages are shown for validation failures.
confirmed_business_rules: Email must be unique across all user accounts.
Password length must be between 8 and 12 characters.
Password must include at least one number, one special character, and one alphabet character.
validation_rules: The system shall validate that the email is unique before creating the account.
The system shall validate that the password meets the minimum length of 8 characters.
The system shall validate that the password does not exceed the maximum length of 12 characters.
The system shall validate that the password contains at least one number, one special character, and one alphabet character.
The system shall validate that the email does not exceed the maximum length of 255 characters.
The system shall display meaningful inline error messages for validation failures (e.g., duplicate email, short password, invalid email format).
open_questions: Handling of leading/trailing whitespace in email or password inputs is not specified (e.g., should it be trimmed or rejected?).
Email format validation rules (e.g., must contain '@' and domain) are not explicitly defined.
Password storage method (e.g., hashing algorithm) is not specified.
Data retention policy for user accounts is not defined.
assumptions: Email verification mechanism (e.g., confirmation link, OTP) will be implemented as a dependency.
A database system will be available to store user account data.
The system will handle password storage securely, though the specific method is not confirmed.
risks: If the email verification mechanism is not reliable or has high latency, users may be unable to log in after registration, leading to poor user experience.
Password storage security (e.g., encryption/hashing) is not specified; if not implemented correctly, it could lead to data breaches.
